summary: Scorching Help>Slip>Franklin and strong Eyes/Attics despite uneven flow
blurb: The show opens with a blazing Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower that puts Jerry on fire. The second set delivers standout moments with Eyes Of The World and Attics Of My Life, though Let It Grow falls flat. Recording quality is mixed, featuring a left‑channel bass drum click and occasional dropouts.
review:
On March 6, 1994 at the Desert Sky Pavilion, the Dead delivered one of those classic early‑'90s nights where brilliance and inconsistency sit side by side. The first set detonates with an incendiary Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower sequence; Jerry’s guitar is hot, his solos soaring, and the trio rides the groove like a freight train. After a few solid staples—Spoonful, Jack‑A‑Roe, Brown Eyed Women—the momentum sputters on Let It Grow, which suffers from both musical lag and digital dropouts that cut short what could have been an epic solo.

The second set rebounds with a tight Easy Answers into Lazy River Road before launching into a powerful Playing In The Band that seamlessly segues into Eyes Of The World. From there the band rolls through Drums‑Space into The Wheel, The Last Time and finally Attics Of My Life, where Vince’s “Stella” keyboards shine and the group stretches out in a heartfelt jam. Good Lovin’ closes the set on a high note, and an encore of Liberty rounds it off. While the performance is uneven—let down by Let It Grow and some rushed moments—the highlights more than compensate, especially for fans craving that early‑'90s Jerry fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Analyses show mixed sentiment: strong praise for the Help>Slip>Franklin sequence and second‑set gems, but consistent criticism of Let It Grow and recording glitches leads to a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Decent soundboard capture that preserves the scorching Help>Slip>Franklin sequence and strong Attics moments despite a left‑channel bass drum click.

key_highlights: Scorching Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower opening
Strong Eyes Of The World and Attics Of My Life jams in the second set
Recording quality mixed: left‑channel bass‑drum click and dropouts on Let It Grow but overall the best SBD source

band_performance.Jerry: Hot guitar tone and soaring solos, especially on Franklin’s Tower; on‑fire throughout first set despite Let It Grow being cut by dropouts.
band_performance.Brent: Vince delivers solid keyboard work, referenced fondly as the “Stella” run, adding texture to the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Bass drum click prominent on left channel, likely due to mix choices; otherwise no specific drumming issues noted.
